reasons: Angela can attach to Esmeralda during a dive, adding shield, healing, and movement support while Esmeralda fights inside the enemy formation.
Esmeralda benefits from support that follows her commit without needing the support hero to stand in the same danger zone.

proof.summary: Esmeralda commits into close-range extended fights, while Angela can attach and add protection during that commit window.
worksBestWhen: Angela saves Heartguard for Esmeralda's real engage.
Esmeralda enters while her team can still follow the dive.
failureCases: Esmeralda dives before Angela is ready.
Esmeralda enters too deep without team follow-up.
The enemy waits out Angela's support window before re-engaging.

reasons: Floryn can support Esmeralda with global healing even when Esmeralda fights away from the main team.
The healing window helps Esmeralda keep pressure during extended frontline trades.

proof.summary: Esmeralda plays long close-range fights, and Floryn can restore her HP globally during the focus window.
worksBestWhen: Floryn holds Bloom until Esmeralda is being focused.
Esmeralda still has enough shield or HP to survive until the heal resolves.
failureCases: Esmeralda is burst down before healing changes the fight.
Floryn uses the global heal before Esmeralda commits.
The enemy isolates Esmeralda after the heal is already spent.

reasons: Estes helps Esmeralda stay in front during grouped fights where she wants repeated damage and shield uptime.
The extra sustain lets Esmeralda hold space longer for her team.

proof.summary: Esmeralda benefits from sustained healing while contesting space near enemies in grouped fights.
worksBestWhen: Esmeralda fights near the team instead of fully isolating herself.
Estes positions safely behind Esmeralda during the teamfight.
failureCases: Esmeralda dives outside Estes' healing range.
Estes is forced away before the fight starts.
The enemy applies burst or anti-heal before Esmeralda can use the sustain.

reasons: Minotaur provides a second frontline body and CC layer so Esmeralda is not the only hero absorbing focus.
His teamfight sustain and disruption give Esmeralda more time to play extended fights.

proof.summary: Minotaur creates frontline space and disruption, giving Esmeralda more time to stay inside the fight.
worksBestWhen: Minotaur engages first or peels after Esmeralda enters.
The team has damage follow-up behind the double frontline.
failureCases: Both frontliners engage without damage follow-up.
Minotaur and Esmeralda split their engage timing.
The enemy ignores the frontline and dives the backline.

reasons: Luo Yi can group and control enemies through Yin-Yang reaction windows, giving Esmeralda better close-range AoE targets.
Esmeralda can enter after Luo Yi creates a clustered fight area.

proof.summary: Luo Yi can create a grouped control window, while Esmeralda follows with close-range AoE pressure.
worksBestWhen: Luo Yi triggers the reaction before Esmeralda commits.
Esmeralda has a path to reach the pulled targets.
failureCases: The enemy spreads out before the reaction triggers.
Esmeralda cannot reach the grouped enemies in time.
Luo Yi uses her control while Esmeralda is not ready to follow.
